task: I’ve got a site incident that needs to be handled before the end of the shift, and there are a few people asking for updates. What should I make sure gets recorded or passed along first so the next steps stay organized and nothing important gets missed?

turns.agent_response:
Record and pass along these items first, in this order:

1. **What happened**
   - Short, factual description of the incident
   - Date, time, and exact location

2. **What was affected**
   - Damage, injury, failed hold point, or anything that will be covered later
   - The **specification / drawing / hold point / requirement** it hits

3. **Photo evidence**
   - Clear photos of the issue
   - Include the location in the photo set if possible

4. **Who was informed**
   - Supervisor, PM, QA/QC, safety, client, or inspector
   - Time each person was notified

5. **Direction given on site**
   - Whether anyone told the crew to **stop or keep going**
   - Who gave that direction

6. **Immediate actions taken**
   - Area made safe, work paused, barricaded, repaired, or left pending
   - Any temporary fix or containment

7. **Related records**
   - Daily report
   - Delivery log
   - Inspection note
   - Any hold-point record tied to that section

8. **Next step / owner**
   - Who is handling it
   - What needs to happen before work resumes or closes out

If you want, I can turn this into a **quick incident update template** you can send to the team right now.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right order. Keep it tight and factual.

What I’d add: **make sure the stop-work call, if there was one, is recorded with who made it and why**. That’s the part people forget later and then everybody starts talking sideways.

So first pass should be:
- what happened
- exact time and spot
- what got hit or held up
- who got notified and when
- whether work was stopped or allowed to continue
- what was done immediately to secure it
- photos
- the next responsible person

And I’d get it into the daily report and any inspection/hold-point note before the shift ends. Otherwise it turns into a memory contest tomorrow.
